pattern: Walking elements in their original order and sieving every multiple of each new value up to the largest group size records, for every possible group value, the smallest index that can ever divide it — a single harmonic-sum sweep instead of checking divisors per group.
hints: For a fixed elements[j], every group value that is a multiple of elements[j] could use it; since we scan elements in original index order, the FIRST time a given multiple gets marked is automatically by the smallest valid index.

Skip an elements[j] value once you've already processed an equal value at an earlier index (later duplicates can never win, they only add work), and skip any elements[j] bigger than every group value (it can't divide any of them).
Precompute, for every integer up to the largest group value, the smallest index (or -1) that divides it using this sieve, then answer each group with one lookup.

edge_inputs.description: single group, single element, minimum sizes
an element larger than every group can never be assigned
duplicate element values, the earliest index must win

stress.description: random groups and elements across the allowed value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 20000)))
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 20000)))
    groups = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    elements = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [groups, elements]
